questions.question:
Consider a non-pipelined machine with \( 6 \) stages; the lengths of each stage are \( \text{20ns, 10ns, 30ns,25ns, 40 ns} \) and \( \text{15ns} \) respectively. Suppose for implementing the pipelining the machine adds \( \text{5 ns} \) of overhead to each stage for clock skew and set up. What is the speed up factor of the pipelining system (ignoring any hazard impact)?



CO & Architecture: NIELIT 2017 DEC Scientist B

We have \( 10 \)-stage pipeline, where the branch target conditions are resolved at stage \( 5 \). How many stalls are there for an incorrectly predicted branch?



CO & Architecture: NIELIT 2017 DEC Scientist B

Comparing the time \( T1 \) taken for a single instruction on a pipelined CPU, with time \( T2 \) taken on a non-pipelined but identical CPU, we can say that ______ ?



CO & Architecture: NIELIT 2017 July Scientist B (CS)
options: \( T1 \) is \( T2 \) plus time taken for one instruction fetch cycle

questions.question:
A pipeline is having speed up factor as \( 10 \) and operating with efficiency of \( 80\%. \) What will be the number of stages in the pipeline?



CO & Architecture: NIELIT 2017 OCT Scientific Assistant A (IT)

A \( 5 \) stage pipelined \( \text{CPU} \) has the following sequence of stages:

  • \( \text{IF} \) – instruction fetch from instruction memory
  • \( \text{RD} \) – Instruction decode and register read
  • \( \text{EX} \) – Execute: \( \text{ALU} \) operation for data and address computation
  • \( \text{MA} \) – Data memory access – for write access, the register read at \( \text{RD} \) state is used.
  • \( \text{WB} \) – Register write back

Consider the following sequence of instructions:

  • \( \text{I1 : L  R0}, loc 1; \text{R0} <= \text{M}[loc1] \)
  • \( \text{I2 : A R0, R0;} \text{R0} <= \text{R0} +\text{R0} \)
  • \( \text{I3 : S R2, R0;} \text{R2} <= \text{R2} – \text{R0} \)

Let each stage take one clock cycle.

What is the number of clock cycles taken to complete the above sequence of instructions starting from the fetch of \( I_1 \)?



CO & Architecture: NIELIT 2022 April Scientist B | Section B | Question: 99
